heading: Information security
label: 18445 Information security

children.label: A
children.text: an update on efforts to implement a system to provide dynamic, comprehensive, real-time information regarding risk of unauthorized remote, proximity, and insider use or access, for all information infrastructure under the responsibility of the chief information officer, and mission-related networks, including contractor networks;

children.label: B
children.text: an assessment of whether the system has demonstrably and quantifiably reduced network risk compared to alternative methods of measuring security; and

children.label: C
children.text: an assessment of the progress that each center and facility has made toward implementing the system.

children.heading: Existing assessments
children.label: 2 Existing assessments
children.text: The assessments required of the Inspector General under section 3545 of title 44 shall evaluate the effectiveness of the system described in this subsection.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Information security awareness and education
children.label: b Information security awareness and education

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In consultation with the Department of Education, other national security agencies, and other agency directorates, the chief information officer shall institute an information security awareness and education program for all operators and users of NASA information infrastructure, with the goal of reducing unauthorized remote, proximity, and insider use or access.

children.label: A
children.text: The program shall include, at a minimum, ongoing classified and unclassified threat-based briefings, and automated exercises and examinations that simulate common attack techniques.

children.label: B
children.text: All agency employees and contractors engaged in the operation or use of agency information infrastructure shall participate in the program.

children.label: C
children.text: Access to NASA information infrastructure shall only be granted to operators and users who regularly satisfy the requirements of the program.

children.label: D
children.text: The chief human capital officer of NASA, in consultation with the chief information officer, shall create a system to reward operators and users of agency information infrastructure for continuous high achievement in the program.

children.heading: Information infrastructure defined
children.label: c Information infrastructure defined
children.text: In this section, the term “information infrastructure” means the underlying framework that information systems and assets rely on to process, transmit, receive, or store information electronically, including programmable electronic devices and communications networks and any associated hardware, software, or data.
